name: Bacteriostatic Water (BAC Water)
summary: Bacteriostatic water is sterile water for injection containing 0.9% benzyl alcohol as a preservative. It is the diluent used to reconstitute lyophilized (freeze-dried) research peptides — it is not a peptide or a therapeutic compound itself. The benzyl alcohol inhibits bacterial growth, which is why a reconstituted vial can be drawn from repeatedly over several weeks (unlike plain sterile water, which is single-use). Tracked here as a supply so you can manage how much diluent you have on hand.
mechanism: Contains 0.9% (9 mg/mL) benzyl alcohol, a bacteriostatic agent that inhibits the growth of common bacteria within a punctured multi-dose vial. This preservative action is what allows repeated withdrawals from a reconstituted peptide vial across a multi-week dosing window.
benefits: Diluent for reconstituting lyophilized peptides (add to the powder to make an injectable solution)
Multi-dose: the benzyl alcohol preservative permits repeated withdrawals over ~28 days
Widely available in 10 mL and 30 mL multi-dose vials
risks: NOT for use in newborns/infants — benzyl alcohol is associated with fatal 'gasping syndrome' in neonates
Not intended for large-volume intravenous fluid replacement
Not for intrathecal or epidural use

dosage.notes: This is diluent VOLUME added per peptide vial during reconstitution — not a dose of anything. More water = lower concentration (easier to measure small doses); less water = higher concentration. Use the reconstitution calculator to pick a volume for your target dose.

halfLife: N/A — diluent, not administered on its own

reconstitution.notes: Not applicable — BAC water IS the reconstitution diluent. Common multi-dose vial sizes are 10 mL and 30 mL.

storage: Store at room temperature before opening. After the first puncture, refrigerate and discard after 28 days (the benzyl alcohol preservative loses reliability and contamination risk rises).
contraindications: Neonates and infants (benzyl alcohol toxicity / gasping syndrome)

references.title: Drugs.com — Bacteriostatic Water for Injection (professional monograph)
DailyMed (FDA) — Bacteriostatic Water for Injection labels
